mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an impending recession, triggering negative earnings revisions and risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the trigger fails to precede market drawdowns by at least 15 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit extension and residential construction indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in >50% of out-of-sample instances, or cross-correlation with SPX momentum drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects improving capacity utilization and supply-side recovery, which reduces input cost pressures and drives short-term positive equity momentum over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in >60% of instances, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflation combined with accommodative policy creates a soft landing environment that compresses equity risk premiums and boosts valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative or flat 60-day forward returns in the majority of historical instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth directly scales corporate earnings expectations, producing a persistent positive relationship with 20-day forward equity returns as analysts update consensus forecasts.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20d SPX returns is consistently below 0.1, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and stable unemployment indicate a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and equity risk premiums, driving short-term positive returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal yields negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing market contraction signals broader economic slowdown and credit tightening, leading to negative earnings revisions and declining S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Housing starts decline does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or the signal exhibits significant look-ahead bias in backtesting.
